educational_objective: Shared looking at faces and simple expressions; copying and naming without long talks.
age_stage_nuance: At 10–12 months faces still lead; simple expression copying begins before long feelings stories.
what_to_look_for: Board books, clear faces, mirror or photo pages, short length.
what_to_avoid: Classroom emotion charts, KS1 metaphor books, sound overload.
methodology: Benched Penguin and Bookshop face and expression board books. Age overlap captured as 0–36m board book range. Ranked face clarity then expression copying then adjacent flap/touch face books to depth 10. URL-verified buyable 2026-07-24.
buying_factor_memo: Face clarity first (Ladybird x2), then photo faces, then expression copying. Flap and touch books fill shelf depth with faces inside known games. Brand cap respected. Overlap with board books card noted for founder.

top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check wear on wooden edges and wheels.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for black and white faces
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A sturdy Ladybird board book of large black and white faces, sized for shared looking and simple pointing on the sofa.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Faces are the social magnet at nearly one. High contrast keeps the look simple while you name eyes and smiles on the sofa. Start here when you want a short face book before busier emotion pages arrive in the basket.
top_picks.ownership_note: High overlap with board books card; rotate rather than double buy.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Publisher/Bookshop buyable where checked 2026-07-24; specialist path due to Amazon soft-404.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Absolute review counts not re-scrapable from bot-blocked mass retail; specialist exemption with written reason.

top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check wear on wooden edges and wheels.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A compact high contrast faces board book with a mirror at the end, made for a final peek at their own face together.
top_picks.ember_verdict: A mirror page turns looking into a short game with their own face. That fits noticing feelings without a talking to. Keep First Focus when you want faces plus one mirror pause at the close of the book. Leave Baby Touch First Focus: out where they can find it again tomorrow.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #2: second Ladybird for mirror; brand cap reached.
top_picks.ownership_note: High overlap with board books card; rotate rather than double buy.

top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check wear on wooden edges and wheels.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A board book of photographs of babies from around the world, inviting slow shared looking at real faces on sturdy pages.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Photos show real eyes and expressions beyond cartoons. You pause and notice together on the sofa. Choose Global Babies when drawn faces are known and you want real faces in the basket. One short go with Global Babies is enough before you tidy away.
top_picks.ownership_note: High overlap with board books card; rotate rather than double buy.

top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check wear on wooden edges and wheels.
top_picks.product_name: Making Faces: A First Book of Emotions
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A board book of clear facial expressions for happy, sad and other feelings, inviting a pause to copy faces together.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Copying a face is how feelings show up before long emotion stories. You pull a face and wait for theirs. Add Making Faces when they watch your mouth in songs and everyday games at home. Stay nearby while they try Making Faces: A First, then stop on a good note.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #4: expression copying is the stage shift beyond plain faces.
top_picks.ownership_note: High overlap with board books card; rotate rather than double buy.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Publisher/Bookshop buyable where checked 2026-07-24; specialist path due to Amazon soft-404.

top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check wear on wooden edges and wheels.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A lift the flap board book where animal faces appear after a pause, linking hide and find with looking at expressions.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Spot is not a feelings textbook, but each flap reveals a face to notice together. That still serves this card. Keep Spot when they already love flaps and you want face noticing inside a game they know. Put Where's Spot? where you both sit most evenings so the invite is easy.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #5: adjacent face noticing via flaps; dedicated face books lead.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A lift the flap board book of zoo animals in crates, each reveal showing a clear animal face after a short wait.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Animal faces after a flap give you something to name and copy. The wait is built in. Add Dear Zoo when Spot is worn and you still want face reveals inside a flap story. Put Dear Zoo where you both sit most evenings so the invite is easy.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #6: second flap face book; Spot ranked higher.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A touchy feely Usborne board book with textures and friendly puppy pages, inviting a pause to look and stroke together.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Texture pages slow you down so faces and fur get a proper look. You wait, stroke, and name. Choose the puppy when they grab fabric and you want faces inside a touch book they can mouth safely. Let them finish the turn with That's Not My Puppy…, then close the activity kindly.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A touchy feely dinosaur board book with textured pages, offering another slow look and stroke game with clear creature faces.
top_picks.ember_verdict: A second Usborne texture book refreshes the same looking habit with bigger creature faces. You still pause for the stroke. Add dinosaur when puppy is known and they point at large animals elsewhere. If interest fades, pack That's Not My Dinosaur… away and try again another day.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A Spot lift the flap farm board book where animal faces appear after each pause, extending the Spot looking game.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Farm animals give new faces inside the Spot rhythm they already know. You keep the pause and change the cast. Add the farm book when home Spot is soft and chewed at the corners. Use Spot Goes to the as the second option when the first favourite is already out.

top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Check wear on wooden edges and wheels.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A classic board book with a friendly caterpillar face and hole pages through fruit, inviting looking pauses while food disappears one bite at a time.
top_picks.ember_verdict: The caterpillar face is simple enough to notice between holes. You pause, look, then poke. Keep Caterpillar when you want a face inside a story that will still earn rereads next year. Keep the session short with The Very Hungry Caterpillar so it stays a friendly invite.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #10: shelf depth classic with a clear character face.
